display_name: How to get ALL ebooks & audiobooks free - even if your library sucks!
author: Matt's Fantasy Book Reviews

parts: Introduction — 250 books read, only paid for ~10
Libby/Overdrive — Free ebooks & audiobooks from local library
How Holds Work — Waitlists, push-back strategy
Reciprocal Library Agreements — Access neighboring systems
State Reciprocity Laws — Get cards statewide (e.g., California)
Orange County Florida Library Card — $125/year, worldwide access
Library Extension (Chrome) — Power user tool for multi-library search

key_takeaways: Libby (by Overdrive) = free ebooks & audiobooks from your local library, sent directly to Kindle or listened in-app
Ebooks borrow for 21 days (some libraries 14 days), audiobooks sync across browser and phone app
Hold strategy: place holds on tons of books, push back delivery date until you're ready to read — waitlists move faster than estimated
Return books early to be a good samaritan — next person in waitlist gets notified immediately
Hoopla = no waitlist alternative, but limited to 4 books/month — check Overdrive first, then Hoopla as backup
Reciprocal library agreements: call neighboring library systems and ask if you can get a card — often gives 3-4x the catalog
State reciprocity laws: some states (California, others) allow you to get a library card at ANY library in the state by showing up with state ID
Pro move: stop at libraries when traveling to collect cards from different systems across the state
Orange County Florida Library System: $125/year non-resident card, available to ANYONE worldwide (including international), enormous catalog
Only need to read ~10 books/year to make $125 card worthwhile vs buying
Library Extension (Chrome extension): shows book availability across ALL your library systems on Goodreads and Amazon pages — one-click borrow from whichever library has it available
Audiobook quality in Libby is identical to Audible — same recordings, speed controls, bookmarks, sync across devices
